model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Houston is one of the elite teams in the entire tournament, ranked 5th nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with a 28-5 record and the nation's 4th-ranked defense (AdjD 91.0-91.9), producing a staggering adjusted efficiency margin of +33.55 compared to Idaho's +1.54 at 145th in KenPom. The roughly 32-point efficiency gap per 100 possessions is enormous, as Idaho's below-average offense (AdjO 108.9, 176th nationally) will be completely overwhelmed by Houston's suffocating Big 12-tested defense, while the Vandals' 107.4 AdjD offers no resistance to Houston's elite attack (AdjO 124.6, 16th nationally). This is one of the most lopsided 2/15 matchups in the bracket, and Houston should advance comfortably.

reasoningStep.title: Houston vs Idaho – 2/15 South Region
reasoningStep.summary: Houston's elite defense and massive efficiency margin advantage make this one of the safest picks in the tournament. The 32-point per-100-possessions gap dwarfs the typical 2/15 margin.
evidence: Houston KenPom #5, AdjEM +33.55; Idaho KenPom #145, AdjEM +1.54
Houston AdjD 91.0 (4th nationally) vs Idaho AdjO 108.9 (176th nationally)
Idaho Torvik #136 with 21-14 record in Big Sky Conference
2-seeds historically defeat 15-seeds approximately 94% of the time
Efficiency margin gap of ~32 points per 100 possessions is well above the typical 2/15 spread

The search tools are unavailable, but I have comprehensive ratings data to make this pick. Let me analyze the matchup.

**Houston (2-seed):**
- KenPom: #5, AdjEM +33.55, AdjO 124.6 (16th), AdjD 91.0 (elite, top 5)
- Torvik: #5, AdjOE 125.1 (13th), AdjDE 91.9 (4th)
- Record: 28-5, Big 12
- One of the best defenses in the entire country

**Idaho (15-seed):**
- KenPom: #145, AdjEM +1.54, AdjO 108.9 (176th), AdjD 107.4
- Torvik: #136, AdjOE 108.8 (166th), AdjDE 106.2 (122nd)
- Record: 21-14, Big Sky
- Mediocre on both ends of the floor

The efficiency margin gap is **+32 points per 100 possessions** — an enormous chasm. Houston's defense, ranked 4th nationally, will suffocate Idaho's 176th-ranked offense. Historically, 2-seeds beat 15-seeds roughly 94% of the time, and this matchup is more lopsided than the typical 2/15 game.
